articles.headline: India Unveils 'Green Hydrogen Hubs' Initiative to Accelerate Net-Zero Transition
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the establishment of 'Green Hydrogen Hubs' across select regions, aiming to boost production and demand for green hydrogen.
• This initiative is a crucial step under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on creating integrated ecosystems for hydrogen value chain development.
• The hubs will facilitate infrastructure for production, storage, and transportation, attracting significant private investment and technological partnerships.
• It seeks to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to its net-zero targets by 2070.
• Skill development programs and R&D support are integral components, ensuring a robust talent pipeline and technological advancement.
gsSubTopics: Green Hydrogen Mission
Renewable Energy
Climate Change Mitigation
Infrastructure Development
Energy Security
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Target to produce 5 MMT of green hydrogen by 2030.
India's net-zero target year is 2070.
Green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Scheme focuses on demand creation, production, R&D, and pilot projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy for energy transition and achieving climate targets through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic benefits and challenges of establishing green hydrogen hubs, including job creation and technological hurdles.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India becoming a major green hydrogen producer and exporter.
Examine the role of government policy and private investment in scaling up green hydrogen production.
Address the environmental benefits of green hydrogen in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: India aims to reduce fossil fuel imports worth over ₹1 lakh crore and abate nearly 50 MMT of annual GHG emissions by 2030 through the Green Hydrogen Mission.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Enter Critical Phase, Focus on Market Access and IPR
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have advanced significantly, with both sides intensifying discussions on key contentious issues.
• Major sticking points include market access for agricultural products, automotive tariffs, and robust intellectual property rights (IPR) protection.
• The FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, potentially creating one of the world's largest free trade zones.
• India is pushing for greater mobility for its professionals, while the EU seeks reduced tariffs on its exports and non-tariff barrier removal.
• A successful agreement could diversify supply chains and enhance strategic partnership between India and the EU.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
International institutions
prelimsPoints: India and EU resumed FTA negotiations in June 2022.
EU is India's second-largest trading partner.
India's key demands include services liberalization and geographical indications (GIs).
EU's demands include tariff reduction on wines, spirits, and automobiles.
The proposed FTA also covers investment protection and geographical indications.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-EU FTA for India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for Indian industries in a post-FTA scenario with the EU.
Evaluate the potential impact of IPR clauses on India's pharmaceutical and technology sectors.
Examine how the FTA could contribute to India's broader foreign policy objectives and global standing.
Suggest measures India could take to maximize gains and mitigate risks from the FTA.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: The EU is a bloc of 27 member states, and collectively, it is the world's largest trading bloc.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Revisit Scope of Digital Privacy in Data Protection Framework
articles.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court has decided to revisit the interpretation of digital privacy rights in the context of India's evolving data protection laws.
• This move follows several petitions challenging aspects of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 and other privacy-related government actions.
• The court will examine the balance between individual privacy, state surveillance, and the legitimate interests of businesses in data processing.
• Key issues include consent mechanisms, data localization requirements, and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• The ruling is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, government agencies, and individual citizens' fundamental rights.
gsSubTopics: Fundamental Rights
Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Cyber Security
prelimsPoints: K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 passed by Parliament in August 2023.
The Act applies to digital personal data processed within India.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
Key principles include consent, data minimization, and purpose limitation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional basis of the right to privacy in India and its evolution through judicial pronouncements.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security, economic interests, and individual digital privacy in the Indian context.
Evaluate the effectiveness and potential shortcomings of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 in safeguarding citizen's data.
Examine the role of the judiciary in interpreting and enforcing fundamental rights in the digital age.
Suggest reforms or amendments to the data protection framework to address emerging concerns.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and a digital lock icon, symbolizing justice and data security.
articles.trivia: The 'Puttaswamy case' involved a retired Karnataka High Court judge challenging the Aadhaar scheme on privacy grounds.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.

articles.headline: Delayed Monsoon Retreat Raises Concerns for Rabi Crop Sowing, Government Assesses Impact
articles.explainer:
• India is experiencing an unusually delayed monsoon retreat, particularly in southern and eastern regions, impacting agricultural cycles.
• The extended rainfall and subsequent soil moisture conditions are posing challenges for the timely sowing of Rabi crops like wheat, mustard, and pulses.
• The Ministry of Agriculture is closely monitoring the situation, assessing potential yield impacts and preparing contingency plans for farmers.
• While some areas benefit from increased groundwater recharge, others face risks of crop damage from excessive moisture and pest infestations.
• This highlights the growing vulnerability of Indian agriculture to climate variability and the need for climate-resilient farming practices.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Effects of liberalization on the economy
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Indian Economy
Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Rabi crops are sown in winter (October-December) and harvested in spring (April-June).
Major Rabi crops: Wheat, barley, oats, gram, mustard, linseed.
Southwest Monsoon typically retreats from Rajasthan by September end.
El Niño and La Niña phenomena significantly influence monsoon patterns.
Delayed retreat can lead to increased pest attacks and fungal diseases.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted impact of climate change and monsoon variability on Indian agriculture and food security.
Discuss government initiatives and policy measures to mitigate the risks faced by farmers due to erratic weather patterns.
Examine the importance of crop diversification and climate-resilient farming techniques in enhancing agricultural sustainability.
Evaluate the socio-economic implications of delayed monsoon retreat on rural livelihoods and agricultural supply chains.
Suggest long-term strategies for adapting Indian agriculture to changing climatic conditions.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer examining soil in a field, with cloudy skies indicating unusual weather.
articles.trivia: The word 'Rabi' is derived from the Arabic word for 'spring', as these crops are harvested in spring.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Disaster Management
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a new generation Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Sriharikota.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging capabilities and advanced sensors crucial for disaster monitoring, resource mapping, and environmental studies.
• It will provide critical data for early warning systems for floods, cyclones, and droughts, enhancing India's disaster preparedness and response.
• This mission reinforces India's self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to utilizing space assets for societal benefit.
• The data from the satellite will also aid in urban planning, agricultural forecasting, and coastal zone management.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency, headquartered in Bengaluru.
EOS series satellites are designed for various Earth observation applications.
PSLV and GSLV are ISRO's primary launch vehicles.
Remote sensing uses satellite-based sensors to gather information about Earth.
India has a robust constellation of Earth observation satellites, including Resourcesat, Cartosat, and Oceansat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's disaster management capabilities and economic development.
Analyze how advancements in space technology contribute to national security and strategic autonomy.
Evaluate ISRO's role in promoting scientific research and technological innovation in India.
Examine the ethical and policy challenges associated with satellite data collection and its utilization.
Suggest ways to further leverage space technology for sustainable development goals in India.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space from a launchpad, with a clear blue sky.
articles.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1975.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Conclude Meeting with Focus on Global Debt Restructuring and Digital Taxation
articles.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors concluded their meeting, emphasizing coordinated efforts to address global economic challenges.
• Key discussions revolved around accelerating debt restructuring for vulnerable low-income countries and strengthening the global financial safety net.
• Progress was made on the two-pillar solution for international taxation, particularly on taxing multinational enterprises and digital services.
• Ministers also deliberated on strategies to mitigate inflationary pressures, enhance supply chain resilience, and promote sustainable finance.
• India reiterated its call for equitable access to finance for developing nations and a greater voice for the Global South in international financial institutions.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Indian Economy
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
It works to address major issues related to the global economy.
Common Framework for Debt Treatments beyond the DSSI was established by G20.
OECD/G20 Inclusive Framework on BEPS developed the two-pillar solution for international tax.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and influence in shaping global economic governance through platforms like the G20.
Discuss the challenges posed by global debt distress for developing countries and the effectiveness of current debt relief mechanisms.
Evaluate the implications of a global digital taxation framework for India's economy and its digital sector.
Examine the importance of multilateral cooperation in addressing transnational economic challenges such as inflation and supply chain disruptions.
Suggest how India can further advocate for the interests of the Global South within the G20 framework.
articles.imageDescription: The G20 logo displayed prominently at a conference venue.
articles.trivia: The G20 was established in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Reviews NEP 2020 Progress, Emphasizes Skill Development and Multilingualism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's implementation across states and institutions.
• The review highlighted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy, as well as efforts to integrate vocational education from school levels.
• Emphasis was placed on promoting multilingualism in education, encouraging teaching in mother tongue/local languages up to Grade 5.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, teacher training requirements, and ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to achieving the policy's goals of holistic development, critical thinking, and skill-based learning.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Focus on mother tongue/local language as medium of instruction till Grade 5.
Aims to achieve 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in preschool to secondary level by 2030.
Establishes a National Research Foundation (NRF) and National Higher Education Regulatory Council (NHERC).
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reforming India's education system and human resource development.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a comprehensive education policy like NEP 2020 across diverse states and socio-economic contexts.
Evaluate the importance of skill development and vocational education in making India's workforce future-ready.
Examine the role of multilingualism in promoting inclusive education and preserving cultural heritage.
Suggest strategies to overcome infrastructure and teacher training deficits for effective NEP implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a classroom engaged in learning activities, with diverse educational materials.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 aims to increase public investment in education to 6% of GDP.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Projects Amidst Geopolitical Shifts
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government has significantly ramped up construction and upgradation of border infrastructure, including roads, bridges, and tunnels.
• This acceleration is a direct response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and security challenges along India's northern and eastern borders.
• The projects aim to enhance troop mobility, facilitate rapid deployment of resources, and improve connectivity for border populations.
• Strategic initiatives like the Border Area Development Programme (BADP) are being strengthened to integrate border communities into national development.
• These efforts underscore India's commitment to securing its territorial integrity and boosting its defensive capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: BRO is responsible for road construction and maintenance in border areas.
BADP aims to meet the special development needs of people living in remote and inaccessible areas near international borders.
Arunachal Pradesh, Ladakh, and Uttarakhand are key focus areas for border infrastructure.
Strategic tunnels like the Sela Tunnel improve all-weather connectivity.
India shares land borders with seven countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic rationale behind India's accelerated border infrastructure development and its implications for national security.
Discuss the challenges in executing large-scale infrastructure projects in difficult terrain and hostile weather conditions.
Evaluate the role of border infrastructure in fostering economic development and integrating border populations.
Examine how geopolitical shifts in the Indo-Pacific region necessitate robust border management and infrastructure.
Suggest measures to enhance the effectiveness and sustainability of border infrastructure projects.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed strategic road winding through mountainous terrain near a border.
articles.trivia: The Atal Tunnel (Rohtang Tunnel) is the longest highway single-tube tunnel above 10,000 feet (3,048 m) in the world.

articles.headline: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Crosses 200 GW Mark, Boosted by Solar and Wind Projects
articles.explainer:
• India has achieved a significant milestone, with its total installed renewable energy capacity surpassing 200 Gigawatts (GW).
• This growth is primarily driven by massive investments in solar power, followed by wind energy and hydro projects.
• The achievement underscores India's commitment to its climate targets and its transition towards a cleaner energy mix.
• Government policies, including production-linked incentives and renewable energy auctions, have played a crucial role in attracting investments.
• The focus now shifts towards grid integration challenges and energy storage solutions to ensure reliable power supply from intermittent sources.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's target is to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Solar energy contributes the largest share to India's renewable energy capacity.
National Solar Mission and National Wind-Solar Hybrid Policy are key initiatives.
Pumped hydro storage and battery energy storage systems are crucial for grid stability.
India is the 4th largest in renewable energy installed capacity globally.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's rapid growth in renewable energy capacity and its implications for energy security.
Discuss the challenges associated with integrating high shares of intermittent renewable energy into the national grid.
Evaluate the role of government policies and international cooperation in accelerating India's renewable energy transition.
Examine the economic and environmental benefits of shifting towards a green energy economy.
Suggest innovative solutions for energy storage and smart grid technologies to support renewable energy expansion.
articles.imageDescription: A vast field of solar panels stretching into the horizon under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: The world's largest renewable energy park, the Khavda Renewable Energy Park, is under construction in Gujarat, India, with a planned capacity of 30 GW.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Healthcare Digitization
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has reported substantial progress in creating a seamless online platform for healthcare services.
• Key achievements include the creation of millions of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) and the digitization of numerous health records.
• ABDM aims to bridge the digital divide in healthcare, ensuring accessible and affordable medical services for all citizens.
• The mission facilitates interoperability between different healthcare providers and promotes data-driven decision-making.
• Challenges remain in ensuring data security, privacy, and universal adoption across diverse healthcare ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched nationwide in September 2021 by the Prime Minister.
It aims to create a digital health ecosystem with unique health IDs for citizens.
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
ABDM builds upon the National Digital Health Blueprint.
It is a voluntary program, allowing citizens to opt-in for health ID creation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in transforming India's healthcare delivery system and achieving universal health coverage.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in leveraging digital technologies for public health, particularly in rural and remote areas.
Evaluate the importance of data security and patient privacy in the context of a digitized healthcare ecosystem.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in scaling up digital health initiatives in India.
Suggest measures to improve the adoption and interoperability of digital health platforms across the country.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected healthcare services through digital devices.
articles.trivia: The ABHA ID is a 14-digit unique identification number that allows users to link their health records across various healthcare providers.

articles.headline: India Submits New Nominations for UNESCO World Heritage Sites, Focus on Cultural Landscapes
articles.explainer:
• India has forwarded a fresh set of nominations to UNESCO for potential inscription as World Heritage Sites, emphasizing its rich cultural and natural heritage.
• The nominations include several cultural landscapes and architectural marvels that reflect India's diverse history and traditions.
• This initiative aims to increase global recognition of India's heritage and boost cultural tourism.
• The process involves detailed documentation, conservation plans, and demonstrating 'Outstanding Universal Value' for each site.
• India currently has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including both cultural and natural categories.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO World Heritage Sites are places of cultural, historical, scientific or other form of significance.
There are 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India (as of July 2024).
The latest additions were Santiniketan and Sacred Ensembles of Hoysalas.
Sites are nominated by member states and evaluated by ICOMOS and IUCN.
The Western Ghats are an example of a natural World Heritage Site in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO World Heritage status for India's cultural preservation and tourism industry.
Analyze the challenges involved in the nomination process and the ongoing conservation of World Heritage Sites in India.
Evaluate the role of cultural landscapes in representing India's unique heritage and promoting sustainable development.
Examine how increased international recognition can contribute to local economies and community engagement.
Suggest strategies for better management and protection of India's heritage sites.
articles.imageDescription: An ancient Indian temple complex set against a lush green landscape.
articles.trivia: The first Indian site to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage list was the Ajanta Caves in 1983.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Phase of National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) with Enhanced Targets
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has rolled out the next phase of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) with more ambitious targets.
• The revised targets aim for a 40% reduction in particulate matter (PM2.5 and PM10) concentration in 131 non-attainment cities by 2026.
• The program emphasizes source apportionment studies, technology-based solutions, and public awareness campaigns to combat air pollution.
• Enhanced funding and inter-ministerial coordination are key features of the new phase to ensure effective implementation.
• Focus areas include industrial emissions, vehicular pollution, construction dust, and biomass burning.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NCAP was launched in 2019 by MoEF&CC.
It initially aimed for a 20-30% reduction in PM concentration by 2024.
Non-attainment cities are those that consistently fail to meet national ambient air quality standards.
The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) monitors air quality.
Grants are provided to cities under the 15th Finance Commission for air quality improvement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of the National Clean Air Programme in addressing India's severe air pollution challenge.
Discuss the multi-sectoral approach required to tackle air pollution, including industrial, vehicular, and agricultural sources.
Evaluate the socio-economic and health impacts of air pollution on urban populations in India.
Examine the role of technology, public participation, and inter-state cooperation in achieving clean air targets.
Suggest policy recommendations for strengthening environmental governance and enforcement mechanisms for air quality.
articles.imageDescription: A cityscape shrouded in smog, with a clear sky in the background as a contrast.
articles.trivia: According to WHO, air pollution is responsible for millions of premature deaths globally each year.

articles.headline: Increased Funding Allocated for Quantum Computing Research under National Quantum Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology announced a significant increase in funding for quantum computing research and development.
• This allocation is part of the National Quantum Mission (NQM), aimed at positioning India at the forefront of quantum technologies.
• The funds will support academic institutions, research labs, and startups working on quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
• The mission seeks to develop quantum processors, secure quantum communication networks, and advanced quantum sensors for various applications.
• This strategic investment is crucial for national security, scientific advancement, and future economic competitiveness.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: National Quantum Mission (NQM) was approved in April 2023.
It aims to develop quantum technologies and associated applications by 2031.
Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics to solve complex problems.
Quantum key distribution (QKD) provides ultra-secure communication.
Quantum sensors can detect minute changes in physical quantities with high precision.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of quantum computing and its applications across various sectors, including healthcare, finance, and defense.
Analyze the strategic importance of the National Quantum Mission for India's technological sovereignty and global leadership in science.
Evaluate the challenges in developing and deploying quantum technologies, including technological hurdles and talent acquisition.
Examine the ethical considerations and policy frameworks required to govern the development and use of quantum computing.
Suggest strategies for fostering a robust quantum ecosystem in India through public-private partnerships and international collaboration.
articles.imageDescription: An abstract visualization of quantum particles and data connections.
articles.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use qubits, which can represent 0, 1, or both simultaneously.

articles.headline: India Actively Participates in IPEF Discussions, Emphasizes Supply Chain Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India continues its active engagement in the Indo-Pacific Economic Framework for Prosperity (IPEF) negotiations, focusing on critical pillars.
• A key emphasis for India is enhancing supply chain resilience, especially for essential goods and critical technologies.
• IPEF aims to strengthen economic ties among member countries across four pillars: trade, supply chains, clean economy, and fair economy.
• India has opted out of the trade pillar but is actively involved in the other three, seeking to safeguard its domestic economic interests.
• Participation in IPEF aligns with India's broader Indo-Pacific strategy to promote an open, inclusive, and rules-based regional order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: IPEF was launched by the US in May 2022.
It includes 14 member countries, accounting for 40% of global GDP.
India joined IPEF but decided to stay out of the trade pillar for now.
Pillar I: Trade, Pillar II: Supply Chains, Pillar III: Clean Economy, Pillar IV: Fair Economy.
IPEF is not a traditional free trade agreement, but a framework for economic cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic rationale for participating in IPEF and its implications for India's Indo-Pacific policy.
Discuss the significance of supply chain resilience in the current global economic landscape and India's efforts to strengthen it.
Evaluate the benefits and drawbacks of India's decision to opt out of the trade pillar of IPEF.
Examine how IPEF contributes to regional stability and economic development in the Indo-Pacific.
Suggest ways India can leverage its participation in IPEF to enhance its economic security and influence.
articles.imageDescription: A map highlighting the Indo-Pacific region, with flags of IPEF member countries.
articles.trivia: The IPEF members include Australia, Brunei Darussalam, Fiji, India, Indonesia, Japan, Republic of Korea, Malaysia, New Zealand, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, United States, and Vietnam.

articles.headline: New Initiatives Launched to Protect Critically Endangered Species in Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has announced new conservation initiatives specifically targeting critically endangered species in the Western Ghats.
• The programs focus on habitat restoration, anti-poaching measures, and community involvement in biodiversity protection.
• Special attention is being given to species like the Malabar civet, Nilgiri Tahr, and various endemic amphibian species.
• Scientific research and monitoring efforts are being intensified to understand population dynamics and ecological requirements.
• These efforts are crucial for preserving the unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Bio-diversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
They are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Home to a significant proportion of India's flora and fauna, many of which are endemic.
Nilgiri Tahr is the state animal of Tamil Nadu.
Malabar civet is listed as 'Critically Endangered' by IUCN.
mainsPoints: Analyze the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Discuss the effectiveness of current conservation strategies and the need for new approaches to protect endangered species.
Evaluate the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in successful wildlife conservation efforts.
Examine the challenges of balancing conservation needs with developmental aspirations in ecologically sensitive regions.
Suggest policy interventions and collaborative models for enhancing biodiversity protection in the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: A lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a waterfall and diverse vegetation.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and influence Indian monsoon weather patterns.

articles.headline: New E-Waste Management Rules 2024 Notified, Strengthening EPR and Recycling Targets
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified new E-Waste Management Rules for 2024, replacing previous regulations.
• The rules significantly strengthen the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework, making producers more accountable for the end-of-life management of their products.
• They introduce higher recycling targets and mandate stricter compliance for manufacturers, refurbishers, and dismantlers of electronic waste.
• The new framework aims to streamline the collection and recycling process, formalize the informal sector, and promote circular economy principles.
• Challenges include inadequate collection infrastructure, low consumer awareness, and the need for advanced recycling technologies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: E-Waste (Management) Rules were first introduced in 2016.
EPR makes producers responsible for managing the disposal of their products.
India is among the top five e-waste generating countries globally.
E-waste contains hazardous substances like lead, mercury, and cadmium.
The new rules aim to formalize the unorganized e-waste recycling sector.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges posed by e-waste generation in India and its environmental and health implications.
Discuss the effectiveness of the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework in promoting sustainable e-waste management.
Evaluate the role of the new E-Waste Management Rules 2024 in transitioning towards a circular economy model.
Examine the socio-economic aspects of the informal e-waste recycling sector and strategies for its integration into the formal system.
Suggest innovative technological solutions and policy incentives for improving e-waste collection, recycling, and resource recovery.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic devices, symbolizing e-waste.
articles.trivia: Only about 17.4% of global e-waste was formally recycled in 2019, with the rest often ending up in landfills or being informally processed.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Shows Strong Revival, Focus on Sustainable and Experiential Tourism
articles.explainer:
• India's tourism sector is witnessing a robust recovery post-pandemic, driven by both domestic and international visitors.
• The government and industry stakeholders are focusing on promoting sustainable tourism practices, including eco-tourism and responsible travel.
• Emphasis is placed on developing experiential tourism, showcasing India's diverse cultural heritage, adventure sports, and wellness offerings.
• Initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and improved connectivity are boosting domestic tourism, while e-visa reforms attract foreign tourists.
• Challenges include infrastructure development in remote areas, skilled workforce availability, and managing environmental impact of tourism.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Tourism sector contributes significantly to India's GDP and employment.
India is home to 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative promotes domestic tourism.
National Tourism Policy aims to develop India as a global tourism destination.
The Incredible India campaign is a global marketing initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the tourism sector in India's economic growth and employment generation.
Discuss the concept of sustainable and experiential tourism and its relevance for India's diverse offerings.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in developing tourism infrastructure and promoting lesser-known destinations.
Examine the impact of government policies and digital initiatives on the revival and growth of the tourism sector.
Suggest measures to enhance India's competitiveness as a global tourism destination while ensuring environmental and cultural preservation.
articles.imageDescription: A group of tourists exploring an ancient monument in India.
articles.trivia: The theme for World Tourism Day 2024 is 'Tourism and Green Investments'.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of Rural Livelihood Schemes, Focus on Women's Empowerment
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development conducted a review of flagship rural livelihood schemes, assessing their impact on poverty reduction and income generation.
• The review highlighted significant progress in empowering rural women through Self-Help Groups (SHGs) under the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM).
• Focus areas for improvement include enhancing market linkages for SHG products, skill upgrading, and access to financial services.
• The government reiterated its commitment to doubling rural incomes and ensuring inclusive growth in rural areas.
• Challenges persist in geographical coverage, convergence of schemes, and ensuring sustainable livelihoods for the most vulnerable.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
keyTerms: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
prelimsPoints: DAY-NRLM is a flagship poverty alleviation program of the Ministry of Rural Development.
It aims to mobilize rural poor households into SHGs and provide them with financial and livelihood support.
SHGs are voluntary associations of people with common socio-economic backgrounds.
The mission aims to cover all rural poor households and ensure their access to financial services.
Women constitute a significant majority of SHG members in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of government schemes like DAY-NRLM in promoting rural livelihoods and women's empowerment in India.
Discuss the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) as catalysts for socio-economic change in rural areas.
Evaluate the challenges in ensuring sustainable livelihoods for vulnerable sections of the rural population.
Examine how financial inclusion and market linkages can further strengthen rural enterprises and SHGs.
Suggest policy measures to enhance the impact and reach of rural livelihood programs, including convergence with other schemes.
articles.imageDescription: A group of rural women engaged in a livelihood activity, smiling.
articles.trivia: The concept of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) originated in India in the 1970s, gaining prominence with NABARD's support.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners Global Attention for Inclusive Growth
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is drawing significant international interest.
• Several developing nations are exploring adopting similar frameworks to accelerate their digital transformation and financial inclusion.
• The DPI model provides a cost-effective and scalable solution for delivering public services, fostering innovation, and boosting economic growth.
• International forums and bilateral discussions are increasingly featuring India's success stories in digital identity, payments, and data exchange.
• Challenges include ensuring data privacy, cybersecurity, and adapting the model to diverse socio-economic contexts globally.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number based on biometric and demographic data.
UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker provides a cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
India's DPI model is often referred to as the 'India Stack'.
G20 has recognized the potential of DPI for global economic transformation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key components and benefits of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model for inclusive growth and governance.
Discuss the global implications and potential for replication of India's DPI in other developing countries.
Evaluate the challenges related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital divide in the context of expanding DPI.
Examine the role of DPI in fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and economic development in India.
Suggest policy frameworks and international cooperation mechanisms to facilitate the responsible adoption of DPI globally.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

highlights: India launches 'Green Hydrogen Hubs' initiative to accelerate net-zero transition and boost green hydrogen production.
India-EU Free Trade Agreement talks enter a critical phase, focusing on market access and intellectual property rights.
Supreme Court to revisit the scope of digital privacy in light of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023.
ISRO successfully launches an advanced Earth Observation Satellite crucial for disaster management and resource mapping.
India's Digital Public Infrastructure model, including Aadhaar and UPI, garners global attention for inclusive growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to produce 5 MMT of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
3. India has set a net-zero target for carbon emissions by 2050.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to achieve 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030. Statement 2 is correct: Green hydrogen is indeed produced through the electrolysis of water, powered by renewable energy sources. Statement 3 is incorrect: India's net-zero target for carbon emissions is set for 2070, not 2050.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for the implementation of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM)?
options: (a) Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Health Authority (NHA) is the apex body responsible for implementing India's flagship public health insurance scheme, Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY), and also for the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023:
1. It applies to the processing of digital personal data within India.
2. It mandates data localization for all categories of personal data.
3. It establishes a Data Protection Board of India for enforcement.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The DPDP Act 2023 applies to the processing of digital personal data within the territory of India. Statement 2 is incorrect: The DPDP Act 2023 does not mandate data localization for all categories of personal data; it allows cross-border transfer to notified countries. Statement 3 is correct: The Act provides for the establishment of a Data Protection Board of India to enforce its provisions.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to India's renewable energy targets, consider the following:
1. India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
2. Solar energy currently contributes the largest share to India's total installed renewable energy capacity.
3. The Khavda Renewable Energy Park in Gujarat is the world's largest operational solar park.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: India has a target of achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030. Statement 2 is correct: Solar energy has surpassed other renewable sources to become the largest contributor to India's installed renewable energy capacity. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Khavda Renewable Energy Park is the world's largest *under construction* renewable energy park, not yet fully operational, and it includes both solar and wind components, not just solar.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four pillars of the Indo-Pacific Economic Framework for Prosperity (IPEF) that India is participating in?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The four pillars of IPEF are Trade, Supply Chains, Clean Economy, and Fair Economy. India is actively participating in the Supply Chains, Clean Economy, and Fair Economy pillars but has opted out of the Trade pillar for now, citing concerns about its impact on its domestic economic interests.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission is a critical step towards achieving its net-zero targets. Discuss the mission's potential to transform India's energy landscape and the challenges in its widespread adoption.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: Energy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and India's net-zero goal. Discuss the potential benefits: energy security, decarbonization, economic growth, and job creation. Analyze challenges such as high production costs, infrastructure development, technological hurdles, and policy support. Conclude with a forward-looking perspective on India's leadership in green energy.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's decision to revisit the scope of digital privacy underscores the evolving nature of fundamental rights in the digital age. Critically analyze the balance between individual privacy, state surveillance, and data-driven innovation in India's data protection framework.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Cyber Security.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the constitutional right to privacy and the context of the DPDP Act 2023. Discuss the challenges of defining privacy in the digital realm, highlighting concerns around state surveillance, data exploitation by corporations, and the need for innovation. Analyze the provisions of the DPDP Act and its limitations. Conclude with suggestions for a robust, balanced framework that protects rights while fostering digital economy.

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate the significance of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model in fostering inclusive growth and governance. What lessons can other developing nations draw from India's experience, and what precautions must be considered for its global replication?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Important International institutions.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI and highlight key components like Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker. Discuss its significance in financial inclusion, efficient public service delivery, and economic innovation. Explain the lessons for developing nations (scalability, cost-effectiveness, interoperability). Address precautions: data privacy, cybersecurity, digital literacy, and adapting to local contexts. Conclude on India's role in promoting digital public goods globally.

quickBytes.text: India's net-zero target for carbon emissions is set for the year 2070.
The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right in India.
Rabi crops are sown in winter and harvested in spring; wheat is a major Rabi crop.
ISRO's PSLV and GSLV are its primary launch vehicles for satellites.
The G20 forum comprises 19 countries and the European Union, addressing global economic issues.
NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for border infrastructure development.
India aims for 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
The Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) is a 14-digit unique health ID.
The Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS1.headline: India Submits New Nominations for UNESCO World Heritage Sites, Focus on Cultural Landscapes
GS1.explainer:
• India has forwarded a fresh set of nominations to UNESCO for potential inscription as World Heritage Sites, emphasizing its rich cultural and natural heritage.
• The nominations include several cultural landscapes and architectural marvels that reflect India's diverse history and traditions.
• This initiative aims to increase global recognition of India's heritage and boost cultural tourism.
• The process involves detailed documentation, conservation plans, and demonstrating 'Outstanding Universal Value' for each site.
• India currently has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including both cultural and natural categories.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO World Heritage Sites are places of cultural, historical, scientific or other form of significance.
There are 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India (as of July 2024).
The latest additions were Santiniketan and Sacred Ensembles of Hoysalas.
Sites are nominated by member states and evaluated by ICOMOS and IUCN.
The Western Ghats are an example of a natural World Heritage Site in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO World Heritage status for India's cultural preservation and tourism industry.
Analyze the challenges involved in the nomination process and the ongoing conservation of World Heritage Sites in India.
Evaluate the role of cultural landscapes in representing India's unique heritage and promoting sustainable development.
Examine how increased international recognition can contribute to local economies and community engagement.
Suggest strategies for better management and protection of India's heritage sites.
GS1.imageDescription: An ancient Indian temple complex set against a lush green landscape.
GS1.trivia: The first Indian site to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage list was the Ajanta Caves in 1983.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Enter Critical Phase, Focus on Market Access and IPR
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have advanced significantly, with both sides intensifying discussions on key contentious issues.
• Major sticking points include market access for agricultural products, automotive tariffs, and robust intellectual property rights (IPR) protection.
• The FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, potentially creating one of the world's largest free trade zones.
• India is pushing for greater mobility for its professionals, while the EU seeks reduced tariffs on its exports and non-tariff barrier removal.
• A successful agreement could diversify supply chains and enhance strategic partnership between India and the EU.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
International institutions
prelimsPoints: India and EU resumed FTA negotiations in June 2022.
EU is India's second-largest trading partner.
India's key demands include services liberalization and geographical indications (GIs).
EU's demands include tariff reduction on wines, spirits, and automobiles.
The proposed FTA also covers investment protection and geographical indications.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-EU FTA for India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for Indian industries in a post-FTA scenario with the EU.
Evaluate the potential impact of IPR clauses on India's pharmaceutical and technology sectors.
Examine how the FTA could contribute to India's broader foreign policy objectives and global standing.
Suggest measures India could take to maximize gains and mitigate risks from the FTA.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The EU is a bloc of 27 member states, and collectively, it is the world's largest trading bloc.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Revisit Scope of Digital Privacy in Data Protection Framework
GS2.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court has decided to revisit the interpretation of digital privacy rights in the context of India's evolving data protection laws.
• This move follows several petitions challenging aspects of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 and other privacy-related government actions.
• The court will examine the balance between individual privacy, state surveillance, and the legitimate interests of businesses in data processing.
• Key issues include consent mechanisms, data localization requirements, and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• The ruling is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, government agencies, and individual citizens' fundamental rights.
gsSubTopics: Fundamental Rights
Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Cyber Security
prelimsPoints: K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 passed by Parliament in August 2023.
The Act applies to digital personal data processed within India.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
Key principles include consent, data minimization, and purpose limitation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional basis of the right to privacy in India and its evolution through judicial pronouncements.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security, economic interests, and individual digital privacy in the Indian context.
Evaluate the effectiveness and potential shortcomings of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 in safeguarding citizen's data.
Examine the role of the judiciary in interpreting and enforcing fundamental rights in the digital age.
Suggest reforms or amendments to the data protection framework to address emerging concerns.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and a digital lock icon, symbolizing justice and data security.
GS2.trivia: The 'Puttaswamy case' involved a retired Karnataka High Court judge challenging the Aadhaar scheme on privacy grounds.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.

GS2.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Conclude Meeting with Focus on Global Debt Restructuring and Digital Taxation
GS2.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors concluded their meeting, emphasizing coordinated efforts to address global economic challenges.
• Key discussions revolved around accelerating debt restructuring for vulnerable low-income countries and strengthening the global financial safety net.
• Progress was made on the two-pillar solution for international taxation, particularly on taxing multinational enterprises and digital services.
• Ministers also deliberated on strategies to mitigate inflationary pressures, enhance supply chain resilience, and promote sustainable finance.
• India reiterated its call for equitable access to finance for developing nations and a greater voice for the Global South in international financial institutions.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Indian Economy
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
It works to address major issues related to the global economy.
Common Framework for Debt Treatments beyond the DSSI was established by G20.
OECD/G20 Inclusive Framework on BEPS developed the two-pillar solution for international tax.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and influence in shaping global economic governance through platforms like the G20.
Discuss the challenges posed by global debt distress for developing countries and the effectiveness of current debt relief mechanisms.
Evaluate the implications of a global digital taxation framework for India's economy and its digital sector.
Examine the importance of multilateral cooperation in addressing transnational economic challenges such as inflation and supply chain disruptions.
Suggest how India can further advocate for the interests of the Global South within the G20 framework.
GS2.imageDescription: The G20 logo displayed prominently at a conference venue.
GS2.trivia: The G20 was established in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Reviews NEP 2020 Progress, Emphasizes Skill Development and Multilingualism
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's implementation across states and institutions.
• The review highlighted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy, as well as efforts to integrate vocational education from school levels.
• Emphasis was placed on promoting multilingualism in education, encouraging teaching in mother tongue/local languages up to Grade 5.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, teacher training requirements, and ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to achieving the policy's goals of holistic development, critical thinking, and skill-based learning.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Focus on mother tongue/local language as medium of instruction till Grade 5.
Aims to achieve 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in preschool to secondary level by 2030.
Establishes a National Research Foundation (NRF) and National Higher Education Regulatory Council (NHERC).
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reforming India's education system and human resource development.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a comprehensive education policy like NEP 2020 across diverse states and socio-economic contexts.
Evaluate the importance of skill development and vocational education in making India's workforce future-ready.
Examine the role of multilingualism in promoting inclusive education and preserving cultural heritage.
Suggest strategies to overcome infrastructure and teacher training deficits for effective NEP implementation.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a classroom engaged in learning activities, with diverse educational materials.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 aims to increase public investment in education to 6% of GDP.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Healthcare Digitization
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has reported substantial progress in creating a seamless online platform for healthcare services.
• Key achievements include the creation of millions of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) and the digitization of numerous health records.
• ABDM aims to bridge the digital divide in healthcare, ensuring accessible and affordable medical services for all citizens.
• The mission facilitates interoperability between different healthcare providers and promotes data-driven decision-making.
• Challenges remain in ensuring data security, privacy, and universal adoption across diverse healthcare ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched nationwide in September 2021 by the Prime Minister.
It aims to create a digital health ecosystem with unique health IDs for citizens.
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
ABDM builds upon the National Digital Health Blueprint.
It is a voluntary program, allowing citizens to opt-in for health ID creation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in transforming India's healthcare delivery system and achieving universal health coverage.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in leveraging digital technologies for public health, particularly in rural and remote areas.
Evaluate the importance of data security and patient privacy in the context of a digitized healthcare ecosystem.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in scaling up digital health initiatives in India.
Suggest measures to improve the adoption and interoperability of digital health platforms across the country.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected healthcare services through digital devices.
GS2.trivia: The ABHA ID is a 14-digit unique identification number that allows users to link their health records across various healthcare providers.

GS2.headline: India Actively Participates in IPEF Discussions, Emphasizes Supply Chain Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• India continues its active engagement in the Indo-Pacific Economic Framework for Prosperity (IPEF) negotiations, focusing on critical pillars.
• A key emphasis for India is enhancing supply chain resilience, especially for essential goods and critical technologies.
• IPEF aims to strengthen economic ties among member countries across four pillars: trade, supply chains, clean economy, and fair economy.
• India has opted out of the trade pillar but is actively involved in the other three, seeking to safeguard its domestic economic interests.
• Participation in IPEF aligns with India's broader Indo-Pacific strategy to promote an open, inclusive, and rules-based regional order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: IPEF was launched by the US in May 2022.
It includes 14 member countries, accounting for 40% of global GDP.
India joined IPEF but decided to stay out of the trade pillar for now.
Pillar I: Trade, Pillar II: Supply Chains, Pillar III: Clean Economy, Pillar IV: Fair Economy.
IPEF is not a traditional free trade agreement, but a framework for economic cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic rationale for participating in IPEF and its implications for India's Indo-Pacific policy.
Discuss the significance of supply chain resilience in the current global economic landscape and India's efforts to strengthen it.
Evaluate the benefits and drawbacks of India's decision to opt out of the trade pillar of IPEF.
Examine how IPEF contributes to regional stability and economic development in the Indo-Pacific.
Suggest ways India can leverage its participation in IPEF to enhance its economic security and influence.
GS2.imageDescription: A map highlighting the Indo-Pacific region, with flags of IPEF member countries.
GS2.trivia: The IPEF members include Australia, Brunei Darussalam, Fiji, India, Indonesia, Japan, Republic of Korea, Malaysia, New Zealand, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, United States, and Vietnam.

GS2.headline: Government Reviews Progress of Rural Livelihood Schemes, Focus on Women's Empowerment
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development conducted a review of flagship rural livelihood schemes, assessing their impact on poverty reduction and income generation.
• The review highlighted significant progress in empowering rural women through Self-Help Groups (SHGs) under the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM).
• Focus areas for improvement include enhancing market linkages for SHG products, skill upgrading, and access to financial services.
• The government reiterated its commitment to doubling rural incomes and ensuring inclusive growth in rural areas.
• Challenges persist in geographical coverage, convergence of schemes, and ensuring sustainable livelihoods for the most vulnerable.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
keyTerms: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
prelimsPoints: DAY-NRLM is a flagship poverty alleviation program of the Ministry of Rural Development.
It aims to mobilize rural poor households into SHGs and provide them with financial and livelihood support.
SHGs are voluntary associations of people with common socio-economic backgrounds.
The mission aims to cover all rural poor households and ensure their access to financial services.
Women constitute a significant majority of SHG members in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of government schemes like DAY-NRLM in promoting rural livelihoods and women's empowerment in India.
Discuss the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) as catalysts for socio-economic change in rural areas.
Evaluate the challenges in ensuring sustainable livelihoods for vulnerable sections of the rural population.
Examine how financial inclusion and market linkages can further strengthen rural enterprises and SHGs.
Suggest policy measures to enhance the impact and reach of rural livelihood programs, including convergence with other schemes.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of rural women engaged in a livelihood activity, smiling.
GS2.trivia: The concept of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) originated in India in the 1970s, gaining prominence with NABARD's support.

GS2.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners Global Attention for Inclusive Growth
GS2.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is drawing significant international interest.
• Several developing nations are exploring adopting similar frameworks to accelerate their digital transformation and financial inclusion.
• The DPI model provides a cost-effective and scalable solution for delivering public services, fostering innovation, and boosting economic growth.
• International forums and bilateral discussions are increasingly featuring India's success stories in digital identity, payments, and data exchange.
• Challenges include ensuring data privacy, cybersecurity, and adapting the model to diverse socio-economic contexts globally.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number based on biometric and demographic data.
UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker provides a cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
India's DPI model is often referred to as the 'India Stack'.
G20 has recognized the potential of DPI for global economic transformation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key components and benefits of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model for inclusive growth and governance.
Discuss the global implications and potential for replication of India's DPI in other developing countries.
Evaluate the challenges related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital divide in the context of expanding DPI.
Examine the role of DPI in fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and economic development in India.
Suggest policy frameworks and international cooperation mechanisms to facilitate the responsible adoption of DPI globally.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
GS2.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'Green Hydrogen Hubs' Initiative to Accelerate Net-Zero Transition
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the establishment of 'Green Hydrogen Hubs' across select regions, aiming to boost production and demand for green hydrogen.
• This initiative is a crucial step under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on creating integrated ecosystems for hydrogen value chain development.
• The hubs will facilitate infrastructure for production, storage, and transportation, attracting significant private investment and technological partnerships.
• It seeks to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to its net-zero targets by 2070.
• Skill development programs and R&D support are integral components, ensuring a robust talent pipeline and technological advancement.
gsSubTopics: Green Hydrogen Mission
Renewable Energy
Climate Change Mitigation
Infrastructure Development
Energy Security
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Target to produce 5 MMT of green hydrogen by 2030.
India's net-zero target year is 2070.
Green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Scheme focuses on demand creation, production, R&D, and pilot projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy for energy transition and achieving climate targets through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic benefits and challenges of establishing green hydrogen hubs, including job creation and technological hurdles.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India becoming a major green hydrogen producer and exporter.
Examine the role of government policy and private investment in scaling up green hydrogen production.
Address the environmental benefits of green hydrogen in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: India aims to reduce fossil fuel imports worth over ₹1 lakh crore and abate nearly 50 MMT of annual GHG emissions by 2030 through the Green Hydrogen Mission.

GS3.headline: Delayed Monsoon Retreat Raises Concerns for Rabi Crop Sowing, Government Assesses Impact
GS3.explainer:
• India is experiencing an unusually delayed monsoon retreat, particularly in southern and eastern regions, impacting agricultural cycles.
• The extended rainfall and subsequent soil moisture conditions are posing challenges for the timely sowing of Rabi crops like wheat, mustard, and pulses.
• The Ministry of Agriculture is closely monitoring the situation, assessing potential yield impacts and preparing contingency plans for farmers.
• While some areas benefit from increased groundwater recharge, others face risks of crop damage from excessive moisture and pest infestations.
• This highlights the growing vulnerability of Indian agriculture to climate variability and the need for climate-resilient farming practices.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Effects of liberalization on the economy
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Indian Economy
Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Rabi crops are sown in winter (October-December) and harvested in spring (April-June).
Major Rabi crops: Wheat, barley, oats, gram, mustard, linseed.
Southwest Monsoon typically retreats from Rajasthan by September end.
El Niño and La Niña phenomena significantly influence monsoon patterns.
Delayed retreat can lead to increased pest attacks and fungal diseases.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted impact of climate change and monsoon variability on Indian agriculture and food security.
Discuss government initiatives and policy measures to mitigate the risks faced by farmers due to erratic weather patterns.
Examine the importance of crop diversification and climate-resilient farming techniques in enhancing agricultural sustainability.
Evaluate the socio-economic implications of delayed monsoon retreat on rural livelihoods and agricultural supply chains.
Suggest long-term strategies for adapting Indian agriculture to changing climatic conditions.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer examining soil in a field, with cloudy skies indicating unusual weather.
GS3.trivia: The word 'Rabi' is derived from the Arabic word for 'spring', as these crops are harvested in spring.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Disaster Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a new generation Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Sriharikota.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging capabilities and advanced sensors crucial for disaster monitoring, resource mapping, and environmental studies.
• It will provide critical data for early warning systems for floods, cyclones, and droughts, enhancing India's disaster preparedness and response.
• This mission reinforces India's self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to utilizing space assets for societal benefit.
• The data from the satellite will also aid in urban planning, agricultural forecasting, and coastal zone management.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency, headquartered in Bengaluru.
EOS series satellites are designed for various Earth observation applications.
PSLV and GSLV are ISRO's primary launch vehicles.
Remote sensing uses satellite-based sensors to gather information about Earth.
India has a robust constellation of Earth observation satellites, including Resourcesat, Cartosat, and Oceansat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's disaster management capabilities and economic development.
Analyze how advancements in space technology contribute to national security and strategic autonomy.
Evaluate ISRO's role in promoting scientific research and technological innovation in India.
Examine the ethical and policy challenges associated with satellite data collection and its utilization.
Suggest ways to further leverage space technology for sustainable development goals in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space from a launchpad, with a clear blue sky.
GS3.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1975.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Projects Amidst Geopolitical Shifts
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian government has significantly ramped up construction and upgradation of border infrastructure, including roads, bridges, and tunnels.
• This acceleration is a direct response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and security challenges along India's northern and eastern borders.
• The projects aim to enhance troop mobility, facilitate rapid deployment of resources, and improve connectivity for border populations.
• Strategic initiatives like the Border Area Development Programme (BADP) are being strengthened to integrate border communities into national development.
• These efforts underscore India's commitment to securing its territorial integrity and boosting its defensive capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: BRO is responsible for road construction and maintenance in border areas.
BADP aims to meet the special development needs of people living in remote and inaccessible areas near international borders.
Arunachal Pradesh, Ladakh, and Uttarakhand are key focus areas for border infrastructure.
Strategic tunnels like the Sela Tunnel improve all-weather connectivity.
India shares land borders with seven countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic rationale behind India's accelerated border infrastructure development and its implications for national security.
